what_it_does: Implements a module-level React store for the board/noticeboard view: it fetches /api/board, normalizes the response into BoardRow objects, holds loading/error/open state, and exposes a useBoard hook that subscribes components to state changes.
observations: Uses a hand-rolled publish/subscribe pattern with a module-level state object and a Set of callbacks.
loadBoard increments a sequence counter to ignore stale responses and maps a flexible API payload to a fixed BoardRow shape.
The exported BoardRow type is narrower than the full API fields described in the header comment (body, history, links, thread, author_session are dropped).
openBoard sets open=true and refreshes data; closeBoard only sets open=false.
ensureStarted is declared but currently a no-op.
Error handling returns a generic user-facing string and sets loading=false; there is no retry loop despite the 'fail-loud with retry' comment.
standouts: Heavy domain framing in comments: FACE-1 breadth surface, DNA boardRecord/boardGroups/renderArchetype pipeline.
Manual pub/sub store instead of Context or an external state library.
conventions: ONE subscribe-store pattern mirrored from channelStore/sessionDrillStore.
Registry-is-truth: branches only on fields returned by the API.
Fail-loud UX: explicit error message rather than silent empty state.
Data/Render separation: store holds raw rows; rendering is DNA's responsibility.
concerns: ensureStarted is an empty stub; its intended side effect is unclear.
BoardRow discards several documented API fields, which may limit downstream consumers.
No request cancellation or cleanup when the board closes or the hook unmounts.
Error message is generic and retry is promised in comments but not implemented.
Type casts (as string) on nullable API fields weaken type safety.
notes: A minimal hand-rolled store that normalizes the board API into a small row type and exposes it through a subscription hook; several comments describe a larger DNA rendering pipeline that lives elsewhere.
questions: What is ensureStarted supposed to initialize?
Where is the retry logic referenced in the fail-loud comment?
Why does BoardRow omit body, history, links, thread, and author_session?
How does the DNA boardRecord adapter consume this store?
purpose_vs_actual: Mostly aligned: it loads and holds board rows and exposes subscription state. The comments promise retry and startup orchestration that are not yet present in the code, so the actual implementation is slightly narrower.
apparent_themes: Minimal global store without external dependencies.
Strict separation between data layer and DNA rendering layer.
intention_signals: The file is trying to be a small, self-contained data substrate for a noticeboard view, deliberately leaving rendering to a DNA adapter pipeline while enforcing honest loading/error behavior.
novelty: The comment vocabulary (FACE-1 breadth surface, DNA organisms, renderArchetype 'board-view') embeds a custom design-system ontology directly into the store file.
contribution: It contributes a board-specific API client, a normalized row type, module-level reactive state, and a React hook that lets any component observe board data without prop drilling.
summary_for_embedding: A hand-rolled React subscription store that fetches and normalizes /api/board rows, tracks loading/error/open state, and exposes a useBoard hook for the noticeboard view.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a minimal, API-truthful data substrate for the board/noticeboard surface while delegating all rendering to a DNA adapter pipeline.

symbols.does: Type describing a normalized board item.
symbols.description: Defines the public row shape the store exposes: id, address, type, state, title, channel, updated, and created. It intentionally omits several fields mentioned in the API comment, such as body, history, links, thread, and author_session.

symbols.does: Type describing the store's full state.
symbols.description: Declares the module-level state shape: an array of BoardRow rows plus loading, error, and open flags. This type is used by the set patch function and the useBoard hook.

symbols.does: Immutably patches module state and notifies subscribers.
symbols.description: Merges a partial update into the module-level state object and iterates over the subscription set, calling every callback. It is the only state mutation path.

Increments a sequence counter to guard against out-of-order responses, fetches the board endpoint, extracts rows from several possible payload shapes, maps each record to a BoardRow, and updates state. On failure it stores a generic user-facing error message.

symbols.does: Opens the board view and refreshes data.
symbols.description: Sets the open flag to true and immediately calls loadBoard so the board is fresh when viewed.
Sets the open flag to false. It does not cancel an in-flight request or clear rows.

symbols.does: Startup guard that currently does nothing.
symbols.description: Checks a module-level started flag and returns immediately. The intended initialization behavior is not implemented, leaving it as a placeholder.

symbols.does: React hook that subscribes to board state.
symbols.description: Registers a callback that forces a re-render via useState, adds it to the subscription set on mount, removes it on unmount, and returns the current module-level state. It does not trigger an initial fetch by itself.

symbols.does: Local callback that forces the hook to re-render.
symbols.description: Created inside useBoard, this closure increments a local counter to trigger a React re-render whenever the store publishes a change.
